learner_goal: I want to explain why vaccines work.
launch_attempt: Vaccines show the immune system a safer preview so it can react faster later.
cold_attempt: A vaccine presents antigen safely and the immune system keeps memory cells.
repair: Memory cells preserve the faster response after the safe antigen preview.
gap_attempt: The repaired link is that memory cells connect the first safe exposure to the faster later response.
spaced_attempt: A vaccine safely presents antigen, immune cells that match it expand, memory cells remain, and those memory cells respond faster during a later infection.
